feat(mobile-ux): premium touch interactions & visual polish for mobile/tablet

All changes scoped to ≤ 1023 px — desktop layout completely unchanged.

• Production card info block: compact padding + 0.93 rem title size.

commit.message:
Change reservation stats to show appointments by scheduled date, not booking date

Issue: Dashboard showed 103 "حجوزات الشهر" (This Month's Reservations) but user only has 8-44 actual appointments.

Root cause: Was using `received_at` (when booking was made) instead of `scheduled_at` (when appointment is scheduled for). All 103 bookings were made in March, but only 44 are scheduled to happen in March.

Fix: Changed all date-based reservation queries to use `scheduled_at`:
- Today's reservations: appointments happening today
- This week's reservations: appointments happening this week (Saturday-Friday)
- This month's reservations: appointments happening this month
- Daily trend chart: appointments per day (not bookings made per day)

This makes more sense for a management dashboard - showing what appointments they have coming up, not when people made their bookings.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Remove cryptography dependency: native C Ed25519, AES-256-GCM, HKDF (#84)

* Native C backends: Ed25519, AES-256-GCM, MSVC consttime barrier

- Ed25519 native implementation with zero external dependencies
- AES-256-GCM native C backend (FIPS-compliant)
- MSVC __asm__ __volatile__ → _ReadWriteBarrier() fix in ama_consttime.c
- AMA_API DLL export/import macros for Windows builds
- CMakeLists.txt MSVC compiler flags

https://claude.ai/code/session_019FDKGqizGCbwC1tzydP9Y4

* Fix CI: MSVC AVX2 flag, black formatting, version pinning

- CMakeLists.txt: Use /arch:AVX2 on MSVC instead of -mavx2 (GCC-only flag)
- tests/test_lazy_imports.py: Fix black 24.10.0 formatting for multiline strings
- requirements-dev.txt: Pin black==24.10.0 (was >=23.0.0, inconsistent with CI)
- setup.cfg: Pin black==24.10.0 (was >=23.0.0, inconsistent with CI)

https://claude.ai/code/session_019FDKGqizGCbwC1tzydP9Y4

* Fix CI: add native C library build to benchmark-regression job

The benchmark runner imports code_guardian_secure and pqc_backends
which require the native C library. Without building it first,
the job fails with RuntimeError at import time.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019FDKGqizGCbwC1tzydP9Y4

* Fix CI: black 3.8 compat, Windows extension build, mypy, formatting

Root causes identified from actual CI logs:
- black==24.10.0 requires Python >=3.9, breaking all Python 3.8 jobs
  (requirements-dev.txt, pyproject.toml, setup.cfg, setup.py)
- setup.py registered ama_core.c as Python extension without PyInit_*
  symbol, causing MSVC link failure on all Windows builds
- mypy warn_unused_ignores flagged type:ignore in ama_cryptography_monitor.py
- test_lazy_imports.py formatted by black 26.x instead of 24.10.0

Fixes:
- Add python_version >= "3.9" marker to black in all dep specs
- Remove bogus _core C extension from setup.py (cmake builds the native lib)
- Wrap Cython extension build in try-except for resilience
- Remove unused type:ignore comment in ama_cryptography_monitor.py
- Reformat test_lazy_imports.py with black 24.10.0 (not 26.x)

https://claude.ai/code/session_019FDKGqizGCbwC1tzydP9Y4

* Address review findings: correctness, docs, and migration safety

1. setup.py: Remove cryptography>=41.0.0 from install_requires — was
   contradicting zero-dep design in pyproject.toml (Devin review)

2. crypto_api.py: get_pqc_capabilities() now reports ED25519 and
   HYBRID_SIG availability based on actual native backend state
   instead of hardcoded True (Devin + Copilot review)

3. crypto_api.py: AESGCMProvider docstring no longer claims PyCA
   fallback — it requires native C only (Copilot review)

4. code_guardian_secure.py: All RuntimeError messages now reference
   native C library build instructions instead of "cryptography
   library required" (Copilot review)

5. key_management.py: Added explicit AES-256-CFB migration error with
   clear re-store instructions, plus ciphertext length validation
   before ct||tag split (Devin + Copilot review)

6. pqc_backends.py: Docstring for native_aes256_gcm_decrypt corrected
   to document ValueError (not SecurityWarning) for tag verification
   failure (Copilot review)

https://claude.ai/code/session_019FDKGqizGCbwC1tzydP9Y4

* Fix tests: update error message assertions for native C backend

Tests were matching on old "cryptography library required" text
after code_guardian_secure.py messages were updated to reference
the native C library build instructions.

https://claude.ai/code/session_019FDKGqizGCbwC1tzydP9Y4

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: AMA-Cryptography
repository.description: AMA Cryptography is a secure, multi-language cryptographic system built on hybrid Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 (Dilithium). Six defense layers with real-time 3R anomaly monitoring protect people, data, and networks against classical and quantum threats.

feat: pre-route weather queries with real-time API data injection

weather tool — the data is already in context for the LLM to format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(autodock): correct PDBQT receptor column alignment for Vina 1.2.5

Vina 1.2.5 requires charge at cols 71-76 (6 chars) and atom type at
cols 78-79. The previous format used only 2 leading spaces, placing
the charge at cols 69-73 so Vina read "0.000 NA" as a single charge
string and rejected it.

Fix: use 4 leading spaces + f"{0.0:6.3f}" (" 0.000", 6 chars) to
align the charge field correctly. Atom type is left-justified in 2
chars at cols 78-79.

Also remove cached 3EML.pdbqt so it is regenerated with correct format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- classify_sentence() (from exporters.py)

commit.message:
Change keep_alive from -1 to 300 seconds (5 minutes)

Setting keep_alive=-1 keeps models loaded forever, causing VRAM waste
and zombie processes. With 300s, model unloads after 5 minutes of
inactivity but stays loaded during active conversations.

This prevents accumulation of multiple model instances in VRAM while
maintaining good response times for bot queries.

and deduplication against existing PRDs.

commit.message:
Sync vote count and timer across browser sessions

When the same user opens the room in both private and regular browser,
votes used in one session now reflect in the other. Two mechanisms:

1. applySongs derives votesUsed from actual vote data on every song
   poll (every 5s), so the counter updates whenever server data arrives.

2. Every 30s, the poll also refreshes guest status from /guest endpoint,
   syncing the authoritative votesUsed and lastVoteReset. This catches
   edge cases where voted songs scrolled off the visible queue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(renovate): add replaceString and migrate fileMatch

Without replaceString, Renovate correctly detects image references but
fails during the file-update phase because it has no template for
reconstructing the image line (especially for first-time digest pinning
where there is no existing @sha256: to replace).

replaceString provides the explicit template:
  image = "{depName}:{currentValue}@{newDigest}"

Also renames fileMatch -> managerFilePatterns to match the current
Renovate API and stop the auto-migration warning on every run.

allow simplifier to adapt tests during structural refactoring

Replaces the blanket "never modify test files" rule with a two-tier
policy: mechanical simplification still can't touch tests, but structural
refactoring (concept compression, module consolidation, API surface
reduction) can adapt tests under strict guardrails — assertion count
must not decrease, no test deletion, mechanical changes only, behavioral
snapshot before/after, and mandatory diff review in the report.

Updates enforcing-tdd skill to explicitly bless test adaptation during
the refactor step as valid TDD practice.

to unlimited categories on Pro.

Updated across: monetization plan, trial expiry UX spec, paywall screen,
and paywall test.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01C2YdBDGaZ1nAWVXCed2Afj

* Implement trial-to-free transition UX

Add complete downgrade flow when users transition from trial/premium to free tier:

- DowngradeService: Applies free-tier limits (8 categories, 1 savings goal)
  by pausing excess items without deleting data
- TrialExpiredBottomSheet: One-time informational bottom sheet shown on first
  app open after trial expires, with options to upgrade, manage categories,
  or continue on free plan
- LimitReachedDialog: AlertDialogs for when users try to activate/create
  items beyond free-tier limits, with upgrade and "Create as Paused" options
- SavingsGoalsScreen: Paused goal indicators (lock icon, PRO badge, muted
  colors, desaturated progress bars), sorted active-first, limit checks
  on toggle and create operations
- SettingsScreen: Expenses section header shows active/limit count on free
  tier, grouped active/paused sections, limit checks on Switch toggle and
  add expense operations
- MoreScreen: Subscription tile shows plan name, limits, paused item count
- Dashboard SavingsGoalCard: Shows "+N paused goals" note when applicable
- SubscriptionState: Added justDowngraded computed property

  tracking with reset on upgrade
